assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend gives rise to a recognised foreign-currency monetary receivable or payable once recorded.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39, IFRS 9, IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the standalone accounting model for exchange differences on foreign-currency monetary items when hedge accounting is not applied.
